vllm.title: [Bugfix] Make image processor respect `mm_processor_kwargs` for Qwen2-VL
vllm.author: li-plus
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Without this PR, if one wants to pass more images with lower resolution to Qwen2-VL models (e.g. 127 images with 256 tokens each):
from vllm import LLM

llm = LLM(
    model="Qwen/Qwen2-VL-7B-Instruct",
    limit_mm_per_prompt={"image": 127},
    mm_processor_kwargs={'max_pixels': 256 * 28 * 28},
)

vllm.title: [Misc] Add Gamma-Distribution Request Generation Support for Serving Benchmark.
vllm.author: spliii
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Adding support for requests generation based on the gamma distribution in the serving benchmark, better aligning the benchmarking process with real-world scenarios and performance characteristics.
Note that a lower burstiness results in more bursty requests.
FIX #10077

vllm.title: [Feature] [Spec decode]: Combine chunked prefill with speculative decoding
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Hey, this PR implements #5016.
The main idea is to make use of the current Speculative Decoder workflow and integrate it with mixed prefill-decode batches.
In particular, we can run the batched prefills and decodes together through the scorer (with the usual prefill|decode layout supported by backen

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Always run mypy
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
If you would like to configure the repo to block auto-merges if the
mypy workflow fails, you will first need to merge this PR so that the
workflow runs on all PRs, regardless of the files that have changed.
This is because github configuration does not support blocking on
workflows that run conditio

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Automate PR body text cleanup
vllm.author: russellb

Add a github workflow that will prune tempalte text from PRs. This
will help ensure that the PR body is appropriate for inclusion in the
squashed commit when a PR is merged.

vllm.title: Online video support for VLMs
vllm.author: litianjian
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Online video support for VLMs
vLLM already supports a large number of MultiModal Machine Learning visual models, some of which support image and video input，such as Qwen2-VL, LLaVA-Onevision, etc. Referring to the implementation of image, this proposal adds support for video.

vllm.title: [Misc] Add environment variables collection in collect_env.py tool
vllm.author: ycool
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add environment variables collection in collect_env.py tool
Many user problems are related to environment variables (https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/serving/env_vars.html#environment-variables). By integrating this info in to collect_env.py tool, make it easier to debug user problems.
Here is one sam

vllm.title: [V1] Add all_token_ids attribute to Request
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds the all_token_ids to the Request class, which is always updated with output_token_ids. Having all_token_ids can be useful because we can directly on the entire list of token ids (i.e., prompt_token_ids + output_token_ids) without the O(n) computation every time.
To make sure that the tw

vllm.title: [V1] Prefix caching (take 2)
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds prefix caching to V1 (take 2). Take 1 is in #9668.
The main difference in take 2 is we adopt a custom doubly linked list to operate free blocks with eviction. This doubly linked list has the following features over the Python builtin deque:

It supports .remove() operator in O(1) time.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Give PR cleanup job PR write access
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When I ran this job on my own fork, it had the necessary permissions
to edit my own PR. Here, we need to explicitly grant the workflow PR
write access.
Since we are giving the GITHUB_TOKEN some level of elevated access, it
is safer to use pull_request_event instead of pull_request. The
difference is

vllm.title: Fixes a typo about 'max_decode_seq_len' which causes crashes with cuda graph.
vllm.author: sighingnow
vllm.bodyExcerpt: There's a typo and causes sequences that longer than max_seq_len_to_capture still be treated as cuda graph captured, causing crashes.
